---
title: "About Me"
toc: true
---

Hey there! I'm Laura. 👋

I have a background in systems neuroscience, and my work has spanned from investigating rapid, visually-driven decision making in mice to helping develop a fruit fly visual system connectome. I also enjoy packaging information into interactive web resources for browsing data.

When I'm not at my computer you can find me with my nose in a book, listening to a podcast, running along trails, trying to grow enough basil to make "fresh" pesto year round, narrating an audiobook for my nephew, painting, drawing or making relief prints. I like to think that life's all one big adventure and have lived in the US, UK, Austria and Australia (never noticed how 'Noah's Ark' that collection of countries is...). 

I think science is for everyone and the knowledge gained from research should be accessible to everyone. I think research scientsts would benefit from shared knowledge and better training in data management, workflow generation best practices and thinking about what the best way to share the knoledge gained from research is. I think it's exciting to think about alternative (non-traditional) ways in which scientific knowledge can be shared that best suits the type of data and the people who might want to use it and how the entire research process can be made more transparent and accesible.
